key_insight: A configurable chemical validity operator converts oxidation-state rules into a tunable prior that filters unrealistic AI-generated inorganic compositions and can also guide generation.
engineering_relevance: This makes materials generation more reliable for high-throughput discovery by screening out chemically implausible candidates while retaining promising low-energy compounds.
method: Built on SMACT with a data-informed oxidation-state model and adjustable thresholds
Applied the operator as a filter for generated inorganic crystal compositions
Used the same operator as a reinforcement-learning reward for a latent diffusion model
evidence_signal: Benchmarking six generative models showed most matched stoichiometry but often missed realistic oxidation-state combinations; filtering removed rare oxidation-state cases while preserving low-energy near-hull compounds.
watch_item: Chemical-validity thresholds are tunable, so overly strict settings could exclude novel but valid chemistry and the operator depends on the quality of the underlying oxidation-state data.
paper_title: Chemical filters for ultra-high-throughput materials screening and generation
